# Customer Outreach Plan — {{builder_name_or_company}}

> **Generated by:** Mitchell Agentic Sprint Step 6 ({{date}})
>
> **A note before you send:** This plan was populated from Sprint state. The re-engagement messages quote your interviewees' verbatim language. Replace pseudonyms with real names before sending. The new-contact list is sized for one focused week of outbound; do not pad. Your outreach has a behavioural anchor (the validated theme), not a vague pitch.

---

## Section 1 — Re-engagement messages

The buyers below took costly action on the problem you're now solving. They're the warmest leads in the world. Send these messages this week, in the order they appear.

### {{interviewee_pseudonym_1}} (interview {{N}})

**Verbatim quote that anchors the message:** "{{quote}}"

**Costly-action context:** {{costly_action_detail}}

**Re-engagement message:**

> Subject: I built the thing you described
>
> Hi {{first_name}},
>
> When we talked in {{interview_month}}, you told me "{{verbatim_quote_truncated_to_one_phrase}}" — and you mentioned {{costly_action_summary}}.
>
> I built it. {{one_sentence_describing_the_built_solution}}.
>
> Want to take a look? I can show you the working version in 20 minutes — Wednesday or Friday next week?
>
> {{builder_first_name}}

> Speaker notes: This is template — adapt the tone to your relationship with the buyer. The discipline is: open with their words, not yours. They wrote the brief; you delivered.

## Section 2 — New-contact target list

The 15–25 new buyer profiles below match your locked Vertical Stack ICP and the validated theme from your interviews. They are not warmed up — Section 3's outreach sequence is what does the warming.

### Where to find them

- **Sales Navigator filter:** {{sales_nav_filter_from_step_3}}
- **Communities:** {{five_communities_from_step_3}}

### Target list (15–25 names)

| # | Profile | Where (community / role / company-size pattern) | Why they fit | First-touch hook |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | {{name_or_handle}} | {{location}} | {{one-line ICP fit reason}} | {{anchored_to_validated_theme}} |
| 2 | ... | ... | ... | ... |
| ... | | | | |
| 25 | | | | |

> Speaker notes: Quality beats quantity. 15 high-conviction targets beat 50 low-conviction ones. The first-touch hook is anchored to your validated theme, not a generic pitch. If you can't write a hook that quotes one of your interview themes, that target probably isn't a fit.

---

## Section 3 — Outreach sequence (3-touch cadence)

### Touch 1 — first contact

**Channel:** {{LinkedIn DM | warm intro | community @-mention | cold email}}

**Subject:** {{behaviour-anchored subject line}}

**Message:**

> Hi {{first_name}},
>
> I've been talking to {{count_of_interviews}} {{ICP_role}} folks about {{validated_theme}}. The pattern is consistent — {{one-sentence_summary_of_theme}}.
>
> Curious: have you run into this? I'd value 20 minutes of your perspective. I'm not selling — I'm building, and I want to learn from someone in your seat.
>
> {{builder_first_name}}

> Speaker notes: This is a *learning* outreach, not a *selling* outreach. The goal of Touch 1 is to get a 20-minute call where you listen. The Sprint trained you on Mom Test discipline — apply it.

### Touch 2 — follow-up (5–7 days after Touch 1, only if no response)

**Subject:** Quick follow-up — {{validated_theme}}

> Hi {{first_name}},
>
> Following up — wanted to share one of the verbatim quotes from my interviews that resonated:
>
> "{{strongest_verbatim_quote_from_theme_map}}"
>
> If this doesn't match your experience, no worries. If it does, I'd love your perspective.
>
> {{builder_first_name}}

> Speaker notes: The follow-up uses *another buyer's words*, not yours. This is rare in outbound — most follow-ups repeat the original pitch. You're showing you've done the work.

### Touch 3 — closing ask (5–7 days after Touch 2, only if no response)

**Subject:** Last note — would this help?

> Hi {{first_name}},
>
> One more — and then I'll let you go. I've built the thing my interviewees described, and I'm doing 5 free walkthroughs this week with people in your role. 20 minutes; no slides; you can ask anything.
>
> Worth a slot?
>
> {{builder_first_name}}

> Speaker notes: Touch 3 is the polite escape hatch. If the target says no or doesn't reply, leave them alone. If they say yes, this is the slot where the sales deck (Section 7 next-step ask) becomes real.

---

## Outreach hygiene

- Send Touch 1 to no more than 5 targets per day (so you can respond to replies thoughtfully)
- Track reply rate per first-touch hook variant — your Theme Map gives you ≥3 themes; some hooks land harder
- Stop sending if you hit 30+ messages without a single reply — that's signal to revisit the Vertical Stack or the messaging, not to send more
